title: RSM Applications Guide — How, When, What-For, and What-Not-For
purpose: Consolidated guide to RSM application domains, use cases, capabilities, and documented limitations. Every entry is backed by empirical evidence from the calibration rack or exploratory analyses. This document treats negative findings as equal in value to positive ones — knowing where the instrument fails is as important as knowing where it works.

carrier_causality_gate.rule: RSM can only measure what the carrier elements encode. If the carrier elements are not causally or structurally connected to the target variable, RSM will correctly report orthogonality — and that report is itself a finding.
carrier_causality_gate.implication: Carrier selection is the first-order decision. Wrong carrier → meaningless metrics (T6c). Right carrier → informative metrics (T2a revision). Irrelevant carrier → orthogonality detection (GS-001).

identity_resolution_principle.rule: The carrier resolves at the element level, not below it. Models not serial numbers. Sectors not stocks. Fuel types not power plants. Currencies not individual transactions.
identity_resolution_principle.evidence: T4a (Backblaze model swap), T4b (finance within-sector swap invisible at 1.43×, cross-sector detectable at 22.06×)

detection_not_prediction_default.rule: RSM's proven capability is structural detection and monitoring. Prediction (CL-03) remains ADD* — not disproven, but not proven forward-looking with real-data K_eff. Any prediction claim requires a carrier whose elements are causally upstream of the target.
detection_not_prediction_default.evidence: GS-001 (gold/silver prediction fails), CL-03 status across all rack versions

negative_results_are_results.rule: When RSM reports no structure, no prediction capability, or orthogonality between carrier and target, that is a valid empirical finding — not a failure. It defines the instrument's information boundary.
negative_results_are_results.evidence: GS-001 cross-prediction R²=-28, T5a Backblaze K_eff vs failure rate r=-0.06, T6a white noise Marchenko-Pastur pass, T6b monopoly K_eff=1.15

application_table.description: What RSM can do, with evidence, domain, and conditions

applications.what: Quantify how many independent dimensions drive a ratio-structured system (K_eff, Kaiser k)
applications.when: Any domain where elements sum to a whole and proportions are the observable
applications.how: Build carrier simplex → compute K_eff time series → Kaiser criterion on PCA → report effective dimensionality
applications.evidence: Backblaze K_eff=17.05, OWID K_eff varies by country, Planck K_eff=3.03, Finance K_eff=6.56, EMBER K_eff varies, Gold 19-currency K_eff=18.98

applications.what: Detect when a system's ratio structure undergoes rapid change (TV spike)
applications.when: Monitoring for disruptions, policy changes, market events, equipment failures
applications.how: Track total variation (TV) of carrier over time → flag exceedances above domain-calibrated baseline
applications.evidence: Fukushima 2011: TV 3.1× mean. Ukraine 2022: TV 3.0× mean. Finance COVID sector rotation: TV 3.7× mean. Finance 5% sector shift: detectable at 2.21× daily baseline.
applications.important_nuance: TV sensitivity depends on K_eff and carrier granularity. Low-K_eff systems (finance k=6.56) are MORE sensitive per-element than high-K_eff systems (Backblaze k=17.05). A 5% shift in a 9-sector system is large; a 5% shift in a 31-model fleet is noise.

applications.what: Confirm that a system's ratio structure is NOT changing when it shouldn't be
applications.when: Quality assurance, baseline monitoring, verifying that 'nothing has changed'
applications.how: Compute TV over monitoring period → compare to established stable baselines
applications.evidence: Planck thermal: TV reduced 52% after carrier correction, stable across 1527 ODs. Backblaze Q1 stability: confirmed. Russia EMBER: K_eff=3.86, TV=0.016 over 26 years — diverse but remarkably stable.

applications.what: Detect when the composition of elements changes (model substitution, sector rebalancing) even if aggregate metrics stay the same
applications.when: Supply chain monitoring, portfolio drift detection, fleet management, regulatory compliance
applications.how: Track carrier composition → detect element-level weight shifts via TV and component analysis
applications.evidence: T4a: 100% model swap = 7.3× daily drift, threshold ~75%. T4b: 50% cross-sector rebalancing = 22.06× daily TV. Within-sector swap only 1.43× (correctly invisible at carrier resolution level).
applications.key_insight: Resolves at element level. Sub-element changes (individual stocks within a sector, serial numbers within a model) are invisible by design — this is a feature, not a limitation.

applications.what: Compare the compression profiles and structural signatures of different domains using the same instrument
applications.when: Meta-analysis, framework validation, identifying structural analogies across unrelated fields
applications.how: Run 7-axis SF on each domain → compare Kaiser k, R², split-half stability, missing recovery profiles
applications.evidence: Five domains produce distinct but valid SF profiles. Finance: R²=0.83 at k=2, stability=1.000. Backblaze: R²=0.96 at k=2, stability=0.978. Planck: R²=0.97 at k=4, stability=0.989. Each tells a different structural story with the same measurement language.
applications.confidence: LOCKED — cross-domain amplifier verified and ranked

applications.what: Identify when a time series enters statistically unusual territory relative to its own recent history
applications.when: Commodities monitoring, market state assessment, anomaly flagging
applications.how: Rolling z-score on target variable → classify regimes by exceedance thresholds
applications.evidence: GS-001: 47 regime transitions detected in gold/silver ratio (1978-2026). 74% normal, 16% gold-favored, 10% silver-favored. Current (March 2026): ratio 61.5, 42nd percentile, NORMAL regime, z=-1.8.
applications.confidence: HOLD — this is z-score analysis on the ratio itself, not carrier-driven RSM prediction. Useful context, but attribution should be honest: it's descriptive statistics, not structural prediction.
applications.warning: Do NOT confuse regime detection (describing where the ratio is now) with prediction (forecasting where it will go). These are different capabilities. RSM has the former; the latter requires CL-03 resolution.

applications.what: Provide historical probabilities of ratio mean-reversion from extreme levels
applications.when: Commodities positioning context, risk assessment, strategic planning
applications.how: Identify historical instances of extreme ratio values → compute forward return distributions
applications.evidence: GS-001: Below 50 (silver expensive): ratio reverted UP 75% of time within 12 months, avg +9.3%. Above 80 (gold expensive): ratio reverted DOWN 61% within 12 months, avg -3.3%. Asymmetric: silver-expensive extremes revert more strongly and reliably than gold-expensive extremes.
applications.confidence: HOLD — valid historical statistics, but NOT RSM-derived. These are properties of the ratio time series. Included because they emerged from the RSM investigation and are useful context for the commodities use case.
applications.warning: Past mean-reversion does not guarantee future mean-reversion. These are base rates, not predictions. Present alongside RSM structural findings, but clearly label the source.

applications.what: Determine whether a proposed carrier is structurally valid (non-degenerate, properly ratio-structured) before running any analysis
applications.when: Before any RSM application — this is the mandatory first step
applications.how: Check: K_eff > 2 (not degenerate), simplex constraint holds, no single element dominates (>90%), softmax or proper normalization applied if raw data isn't naturally ratio-structured
applications.evidence: GS-001 v1: absolute gold prices → VND 60.8%, IDR 34.9%, K_eff=2.02, Kaiser k=1 = DEGENERATE. GS-001 v2: softmax returns → K_eff=18.98, even weights = VALID. T6c: fake simplex → superficially valid but meaningless. T2a: Planck carrier with radiation monitors → noisy; without → informative.
domains_proven: all domains — this is the universal gate
applications.confidence: LOCKED — triple proof (T6c + T2a + T4b + GS-001 degenerate carrier example)

what_not_for_table.description: What RSM cannot do or should not be used for, with evidence

limitations.cannot_do: Predict target variables from structurally orthogonal carriers
limitations.what_this_means: If the carrier elements don't cause or structurally encode the target variable, RSM will correctly report no predictive power — cross-prediction R² will be negative (worse than mean)
limitations.evidence: GS-001: gold currency structure → gold/silver ratio, R²=-28. Backblaze T5a: K_eff → failure rate, r=-0.06.
limitations.why: Structure and target are in different information domains (T5-type finding). This is analogous to measuring someone's height to predict their IQ — the measurement instrument works fine, the variables just aren't related.
limitations.what_to_do_instead: For prediction, build a carrier whose elements are causally upstream of the target. For gold/silver: industrial demand indicators, monetary policy signals, speculative positioning — not currency FX structure.

limitations.what_this_means: RSM sees sectors but not individual stocks. Models but not serial numbers. Fuel types but not individual power plants.
limitations.evidence: T4a: serial number swap invisible. T4b: within-sector stock swap at 1.43× baseline (marginally visible, not actionable).
limitations.why: The carrier is a simplex of elements. Each element is an atomic unit. Internal composition of an element is outside the measurement window.
limitations.what_to_do_instead: If you need sub-element resolution, build a new carrier at the finer granularity. E.g., for individual stock monitoring, build a stock-level carrier within a single sector.

limitations.what_this_means: If one or two elements dominate (>95% of carrier weight), K_eff collapses to near 1, Kaiser k=1, and the system is effectively one-dimensional. RSM will run but results are meaningless.
limitations.evidence: GS-001 v1: VND+IDR = 95.7% of carrier, K_eff=2.02, cross-prediction R²=-27.6. T6b monopoly: K_eff=1.15.
limitations.why: A degenerate carrier has no structural diversity to decompose. It's like trying to find patterns in a constant.
limitations.what_to_do_instead: Fix the carrier: use returns instead of levels, use softmax normalization, remove the dominating element, or redefine elements at appropriate scale.

limitations.cannot_do: Predict forward without proven CL-03 gate
limitations.what_this_means: Forward-looking prediction using K_eff or PCA scores to forecast target variables is not yet proven with real-data K_eff. CL-03 status: ADD*.
limitations.evidence: GS-001: forward prediction R² negative at all lead times (3m, 6m, 12m). Direction accuracy 47.8% (below coin flip). CL-03 across all rack versions: ADD* — not disproven, but not confirmed.
limitations.why: Detection ≠ prediction. RSM can tell you what structure looks like NOW and whether it CHANGED. Whether current structure forecasts future target values is an open empirical question.
limitations.what_to_do_instead: Use RSM for detection and monitoring (proven). For prediction applications, clearly label as experimental and validate with out-of-sample forward tests. The gold/silver test showed that even with a valid carrier, prediction can fail if carrier and target are in different information domains.

limitations.cannot_do: Work with non-ratio data without transformation
limitations.what_this_means: RSM requires a simplex carrier where elements sum to 1 (or a constant). Raw absolute data, counts, or unbounded measurements must be transformed.
limitations.evidence: T6c: fake simplex without genuine ratio structure → superficially valid but meaningless. GS-001 v1: absolute prices normalized naively → degenerate carrier due to denomination scale.
limitations.why: The mathematical framework (PCA on simplex, TV as structural drift) assumes compositional data. Violating this assumption doesn't crash the computation — it produces uninterpretable results.
limitations.what_to_do_instead: Transform: softmax for returns, share-of-total for naturally compositional data (energy mix, fleet composition, sector weights), CLR/ILR for general compositional transforms.

limitations.what_this_means: RSM measures structural properties. Interpreting those measurements requires domain knowledge. A TV spike tells you structure changed; it doesn't tell you why.
limitations.evidence: EMBER COVID (TV 1.4×) vs Finance COVID (TV 3.7×) — same event, different structural reading. Without domain knowledge, you wouldn't know that electricity demand preservation explains the difference from financial sector rotation.
limitations.why: RSM is an instrument, not an oracle. Like a thermometer tells you temperature but not why you have a fever.
limitations.what_to_do_instead: Pair RSM outputs with domain-specific analysis. The cross-domain comparison capability is powerful precisely because it reveals where domain-specific forces are at work.

domain_application_matrix.description: Quick-reference: which RSM capabilities are proven in which domains

ember_electricity.shock_detection: PROVEN (Fukushima 3.1×, Ukraine 3.0×, COVID correctly invisible at 1.4×)
ember_electricity.special_finding: COVID invisibility — demand-preserving shocks don't alter ratios, correctly detected as non-structural

planck_satellite.carrier_validity_gate: PROVEN (T2a revision: bad carrier → noisy, corrected → informative)

finance_equities.identity_detection: PROVEN (T4b, 22.06× at 50% cross-sector swap, 5% detectable at 2.21×)

commodities_gold_silver.carrier_validity_gate: PROVEN (v1 degenerate → v2 corrected demonstrates gate)
commodities_gold_silver.prediction_from_currency_carrier: DISPROVEN (direction accuracy 47.8%, all forward R² negative)
special_findings: T5-type orthogonality confirmed in a new domain — currency structure and precious metal relative pricing are in different information domains
Degenerate carrier detection demonstrated: absolute prices create denomination-scale artifacts (VND/IDR domination)
Softmax normalization proven as carrier construction tool for returns data
The negative prediction result is itself a use case: RSM correctly identifies when a carrier lacks predictive power for a given target

btl_loudspeakers.status: NOT YET TESTED — origin domain, awaiting T1c measurements
btl_loudspeakers.expected_carrier: Driver frequency response shares across measurement bands
btl_loudspeakers.expected_capability: Structural complexity of crossover network, driver contribution balance, system identity verification

revelations_from_gold_silver.description: New insights emerged from the commodities investigation that apply to all RSM applications

revelations.title: Orthogonality detection is a capability, not a failure mode
revelations.insight: When RSM reports R²=-28 and direction accuracy below coin flip, that IS the finding. The instrument correctly identified that currency structure and the gold/silver ratio are in different information domains. Users should treat this as actionable intelligence: don't waste effort building trading strategies on currency-structure signals for metal ratio timing.
revelations.general_principle: Before building any RSM application, ask: is my carrier causally upstream of my target? If not, RSM will tell you — and that saves you from false confidence.

revelations.title: Carrier construction is a learnable skill with documented failure modes
revelations.insight: The v1→v2 progression in gold/silver demonstrated a reproducible pattern: absolute levels in mixed-denomination data → degenerate carrier (VND/IDR domination, K_eff=2.02). Returns with softmax → healthy carrier (K_eff=18.98, even weights). This is now a documented recipe for anyone building a multi-currency or multi-unit carrier.
revelations.general_principle: Carrier construction has known pitfalls: denomination scale, level vs returns, element count vs information content. Document these for each new domain.

revelations.title: Detection and prediction are fundamentally different capabilities with different prerequisites
revelations.insight: RSM can detect that the gold/silver ratio is in a statistically unusual regime (z-score method). It cannot predict where the ratio will go next from currency structure. These require different things: detection requires a valid carrier or a well-characterized target series. Prediction requires a carrier that is causally upstream of the target AND forward-looking information content.
revelations.general_principle: Always specify whether an RSM application is for detection (proven, locked) or prediction (ADD*, requires per-application validation).

revelations.title: Mean-reversion statistics are RSM-adjacent, not RSM-derived
revelations.insight: The gold/silver mean-reversion findings (below 50: 75% revert 12m; above 80: 61% revert 12m) emerged from the RSM investigation but are properties of the ratio time series, not structural predictions. Labeling matters: useful context, but don't attribute to RSM what belongs to descriptive statistics.
revelations.general_principle: When RSM investigations produce non-RSM findings, document them separately with honest attribution. The investigation adds value even when the framework isn't the source of every insight.

revelations.title: The T5-type pattern is cross-domain
revelations.insight: Backblaze T5a: K_eff vs failure rate, r=-0.06. Gold/silver: currency structure vs ratio, R²=-28. Same pattern: the structural observable and the target outcome are orthogonal. This is now confirmed in two completely unrelated domains (industrial fleet management and commodities), strengthening the claim that T5 is a general property of ratio-structured systems, not a domain-specific artifact.
revelations.general_principle: Orthogonality between structure and outcome may be the norm, not the exception. This has implications for anyone expecting K_eff or TV to predict external outcomes: the default assumption should be orthogonality until causally-connected carriers are demonstrated.

decision_flowchart.description: Step-by-step decision process for applying RSM to a new domain

steps.question: Is your data naturally ratio-structured (elements sum to a whole)?
steps.if_no: Transform: softmax for returns, share-of-total for counts, CLR/ILR for general compositional. If no meaningful ratio interpretation exists, RSM may not be appropriate.

steps.question: Build the carrier simplex. Is K_eff > 2 and are weights reasonably distributed?
steps.if_yes: Carrier is non-degenerate. Proceed to step 3.
steps.if_no: Carrier is degenerate. Check for denomination-scale artifacts, single-element domination, or inappropriate normalization. Fix and rebuild. See GS-001 v1→v2 for worked example.

steps.question: What do you want to know?

options.how_complex: Run 7-axis SF → report K_eff, Kaiser k, R² at Kaiser. This is PROVEN across 6 domains.
options.is_something_changing: Track TV over time → compare to calibrated baseline. PROVEN for shock detection.
options.is_everything_stable: Track TV over monitoring period → confirm within stable baseline. PROVEN for stability verification.
options.did_composition_change: Track element-level weights → detect shifts via TV component analysis. PROVEN for identity detection.

steps.question: For prediction: are your carrier elements causally upstream of your target variable?
steps.if_yes: Run cross-prediction test. If R² > 0 and direction accuracy > 55%, you may have a prediction signal. BUT: CL-03 remains ADD* — validate with out-of-sample forward tests. Label as experimental.
steps.if_no: RSM will correctly report orthogonality (negative R², coin-flip direction accuracy). This IS the finding — your carrier doesn't predict your target. Either find a causally connected carrier or accept that prediction isn't available for this carrier-target pair.
steps.if_uncertain: Run the test. If R²<0, that answers the question. The test is cheap; the finding is valuable either way.

steps.question: Do you need to compare across domains?
steps.if_yes: Run identical 7-axis SF on each domain → use cross-domain amplifier (f_rms) to rank and compare compression profiles. This is PROVEN and produces domain-agnostic structural signatures.
steps.if_no: Report domain-specific findings with calibrated confidence levels.

use_case_registry.description: Specific use cases with status and evidence trail

cases.use_case: Monitor fleet composition drift and detect model substitution events
Track energy source diversification and detect policy-driven structural shifts
Detect fuel mix disruptions (geopolitical events, disasters) and distinguish demand shocks from structural shocks
cases.special: COVID invisibility finding — demand-preserving shocks correctly read as non-structural

cases.use_case: Monitor thermal subsystem balance and validate carrier element selection
Detect sector rebalancing events, verify portfolio composition identity, monitor structural stability
cases.special: Sammon & Shim convergence — independent research validates ratio-as-observable

cases.use_case: Monitor gold/silver ratio regime state and provide historical base rates for mean-reversion assessment
cases.what_works: Carrier construction (softmax returns), complexity measurement (K_eff=18.98), regime detection (z-score), mean-reversion base rates (75% revert from below 50 within 12m)
cases.what_does_not_work: Prediction of gold/silver ratio from currency structure (R²=-28, direction 47.8%)
cases.special: First documented case of T5-type orthogonality outside the original Backblaze domain. Negative prediction result is itself a valuable finding.

cases.use_case: Predict commodity price transitions from ratio-structured carriers
cases.status: NOT PROVEN — requires causally connected carrier
cases.what_would_be_needed: A carrier whose elements are causally upstream of the target: industrial demand indicators, monetary policy signals, speculative positioning data. Currency FX structure is not sufficient.
cases.warning: Do NOT use RSM with a currency carrier to predict commodity price ratios. This has been empirically disproven.

cases.use_case: Measure crossover network complexity, driver contribution balance, system identity verification
cases.status: NOT YET TESTED — T1c awaiting physical measurements
cases.note: Origin domain for HUF. Zero data exists. Will be the 16th calibration rack test when complete.

use_rsm_when: You have ratio-structured data (shares, proportions, compositions) and want to measure structural complexity
You need to detect whether a system's composition has changed (shock detection, identity monitoring)
You want to verify that a system's structure is stable over a monitoring period
You want to compare structural signatures across different domains using a common measurement language
You need to determine whether a proposed carrier has predictive power for a specific target (the test itself is informative either way)
do_not_use_rsm_when: Your data is not ratio-structured and cannot be meaningfully transformed to a simplex
You expect prediction from a carrier that is not causally connected to your target (will correctly report orthogonality, but don't confuse the test with the prediction)
You need sub-element resolution (individual stocks, serial numbers, specific power plants)
You have a degenerate system (one element >95% — K_eff ≈ 1, nothing to decompose)
You want an oracle that replaces domain expertise (RSM measures structure; interpretation requires domain knowledge)
always_do_first: Validate carrier: K_eff > 2, no single-element domination, proper ratio normalization
Run the carrier validity gate BEFORE interpreting any other metric
If using returns data, apply softmax normalization to avoid denomination-scale artifacts
Specify whether your application is detection (proven) or prediction (ADD*, requires per-case validation)

corpus_trail.triggered_by: Gold/silver analysis (GS-001) revealing orthogonality detection as a first-class use case, combined with Peter's directive to document all findings as use cases including revelations about how/when/what-for
